satire_attempt: Stand up, says Krishna. Stand up, says the staff engineer's skip-level. The Postgres column will not delete itself. After three sprints of soul-searching about Kafka consumers, our hero finds courage. Truly the divine teaching is: just do the ticket. Subscribe to the newsletter for next week's verse where Krishna also tells us to drop the column.
verdict_reason: My satire only lands by stripping out the verse's actual specificity — that the orbit lasted three sprints, that the concerns did not survive five minutes of investigation, and that the falsifiability rejects the use of the verse to override genuine technical objections. Once those features are restored, 'just do the ticket' is no longer the verse — the verse says 'name the specific failure mode where the concerns are not real.' Mocking a precise diagnosis as if it were 'just do the ticket' requires inventing claims the verse does not make. PASS.
